name: sentinel-harness
//: Zero-setup dev/eval container. Open in GitHub Codespaces or VS Code Dev Containers and you land in a shell where `make test` and `make demo` just work — both fully OFFLINE (no AWS, no credentials, no secrets). The aws-cli + node features are here only for the optional IaC path (`make synth` / `cdk`); nothing in the default flow touches AWS.

features.//uv: uv is this repo's iron rule for Python envs/deps; the Makefile drives everything through `uv run`.
features.//node: Node 20 for the iac-cdk path (cdk synth / tsc), matching .github/workflows/ci.yml.
features.//aws-cli: aws-cli for the optional live L3 IaC path only; no credentials are baked in.

remoteEnv.//: Dummy, non-secret values so imports + the offline suite are hermetic. The 000000000000 account is the repo-wide placeholder; the suite makes ZERO AWS calls. NEVER put real credentials here — Codespaces secrets are the mechanism for that, and only for the opt-in live path.

//postCreate: Warm the caches once so an evaluator's first `make test` / `make demo` is instant: install deps, pre-fetch the CDK npm deps, then run the offline suite as a warmup. `uv` pre-fetches its pinned Python + deps here. Every step is non-fatal — the container must always come up green; the evaluator runs `make test` / `make demo` themselves. NOTE: the 3 `tests/test_prop_*.py` files need `hypothesis`, which the Makefile's `uv run --with` list does not yet request (CI installs it separately), so the warmup runs the suite with `hypothesis` added; `make test` alone skips those 3 files' collection.
postCreateCommand: uv sync --frozen || uv sync; (cd iac-cdk && npm ci) || echo 'cdk deps skipped (optional IaC path)'; uv run --no-project --python 3.13 --with pytest --with boto3 --with pyyaml --with hypothesis --with . python -m pytest tests/ -q || echo 'offline test warmup finished (non-fatal)'
